prompt:
You are a software requirements analyst.
You are given a list of software requirements with their IDs.

Your task is to identify which of the following software requirements are non-functional requirements (NFRs).

Definition:

Functional requirements describe what the system should do, such as features, behaviors, user actions, data operations, or business rules.
Non-functional requirements describe how the system should be, perform, operate, or be constrained. They include quality attributes, system properties, technical constraints, environmental constraints, and operational constraints.

Non-functional requirements include, but are not limited to:

Performance, such as response time, throughput, scalability, capacity, concurrency, or resource usage.
Security, such as access control, authentication, authorization, data protection, integrity, or protection against malicious input.
Maintainability, such as modifiability, configurability, extensibility, code quality, ease of updates, or system evolution.
Usability, such as ease of use, accessibility, learnability, user experience, or user-interface quality expectations.
Reliability, availability, robustness, or fault tolerance.
Compatibility, portability, interoperability, supported platforms, supported environments, or technology constraints.

Important guidelines:

Requirements describing constraints on supported environments, platforms, technologies, compatibility, portability, or interoperability should be considered NFRs.
Requirements about system qualities, system properties, user experience, reliability, availability, or operational constraints should be considered NFRs.
Requirements that only describe a concrete system function, user action, data operation, or business rule should be considered FRs.
If a requirement could reasonably be interpreted as either FR or NFR, classify it based on its primary concern. A behavior, feature, user action, or data operation should be classified as FR. A quality attribute, system property, or explicit constraint should be classified as NFR.
Do not classify a requirement as NFR only because it is vague or difficult to understand.

Instructions:
For each requirement, output exactly one line in the format:
REQ_ID: NFR
or
REQ_ID: FR
Use only the original requirement IDs from the input.
Do not include explanations, summaries, or any extra text.
Do not change the format or add punctuation.

Output format (STRICT):
R1: NFR
R2: FR
R3: NFR
...

Requirements:
1. [R491] The product shall simulate the look of ships at sea.
2. [R492] The product shall display the grids within a circle as a view from a periscope.
3. [R493] The product shall display each type of ship in a grid using an image of the specific type of ship.
4. [R494] When the offensive player takes a shot  the product shall simulate the sound of a ship at sea.
5. [R495] When a shot is marked as a miss  the product shall simulate the sound of a shot missing its target.
6. [R496] When a shot is marked as a hit  the product shall simulate the sound of a shot hitting its target.
7. [R497] When a ship is sunk  the product shall simulate the sound of a sinking ship.
8. [R498] The product shall be available 24 hours per day  seven days per week.
9. [R499] The product shall able to handle a large number of games simultaneously.
10. [R500] The response time shall be fast enough to maintain the flow of the game.  The response time shall be no more than 2 seconds for 95% of responses and no more than 4% for the remaining responses.
11. [R501] The product shall protect the identity of the players. The product shall provide players no access to information that might reveal the identity of another player.
12. [R502] The product shall be intuitive and self-explanatory.  Nine out ten users shall be able to successfully complete a game with no training.
13. [R503] The product shall be available to players who understand English  Spanish and French.  The product shall allow the user to select the language upon accessing the product.
14. [R504] The product shall provide players no access to information that might reveal the identity of another player
15. [R505] The response time shall be fast enough to maintain the flow of the game. The response time shall be no more than 2 seconds for 95% of responses and no more than 4% for the remaining responses.
16. [R506] The product shall maintain a list of players.
17. [R507] The product shall allow a user to identify himself or herself as a player.
18. [R508] Once a game has been initiated  the product shall make the players assigned to the game unavailable for selection from the list of players.
19. [R509] The product shall allow a user to remove himself or herself from the list of players at any time.
20. [R510] The product shall make inactive players unavailable for selection from the list of players.
21. [R511] The product shall remove players who have been inactive for 24 hours from the list of players.
22. [R512] The product shall allow a player to initiate a game by selecting another available player from the list of players.
23. [R513] Once a game is initiated  the product shall display a defensive grid to each player
24. [R514] Once a game is initiated  the product shall display an offensive grid to each player.
25. [R515] The product shall prevent a player from viewing the offensive or defensive grids of the other player.
26. [R516] Once a game is initiated  the product shall allow each player to position their 5 ships on their respective defensive grids.
27. [R517] The product shall allow a player to position ships horizontally on their grid.
28. [R518] The product shall allow the player to position ships vertically on their grid.
29. [R519] The product shall allow the player to position ships diagonally on their grid
30. [R520] The product shall prevent the player from overlapping ships on their grid.
31. [R521] When both players have positioned their 5 ships on their defensive grids  the product shall define the status of each player for the first turn.
32. [R522] For each turn  the product shall identify one player as offensive status and one player as defensive status.
33. [R523] The product shall maintain the status of each player for a turn  either offensive or defensive.
34. [R524] At the start of each turn  the product shall notify each player of his or her status.
35. [R525] For each turn  the product shall allow the offensive player to define a shot.
36. [R526] For each shot  the product shall examine the defensive grid of the defensive player to determine whether the shot was a hit or miss.
37. [R527] For each shot  the product shall notify the players whether the shot was a hit or miss.
38. [R528] For each shot  the product shall mark the shot as a hit or miss on the defensive grid of the defensive player.
39. [R529] For each shot  the product shall mark the shot as a hit or miss on the offensive grid of the offensive player.
40. [R530] When the hit results in sinking a ship  the product shall notify the players that the ship was sunk.
41. [R531] When the hit results in sinking a ship  the product shall mark the ship as sunk on the defensive grid of the defensive player.
42. [R532] When the hit results in sinking a ship  the product shall indicate the type of ship sunk on the defensive grid of the defensive player.
43. [R533] When the hit results in sinking a ship  the product shall mark the ship as sunk on the offensive grid of the offensive player.
44. [R534] If the shot was marked as a hit  the product shall allow the offensive player to define a shot.
45. [R535] If the shot was marked as a miss  the product shall change the status of the offensive player to defensive
46. [R536] If the shot was marked as a miss  the product shall change the status of the defensive player to offensive
47. [R537] The product shall continue to assign turns until the game is ended.
48. [R538] The product shall end a game when a hit by the offensive player results in sinking the last of the defensive players ships  which had not been previously marked as sunk.
49. [R539] The product shall allow a player to end a game at any time during the game.
50. [R540] When a game is ended  the product shall notify the players that the game is ended.
51. [R541] The notification of the game ending will include the reason the game is ended (i.e.  a player has won the game or a player has ended the game).
52. [R542] When a game is ended  the product shall allow each player to identify himself or herself as an available player in the list of players.
53. [R543] When a game is ended  the product shall allow each player to remove himself or herself from the list of players.
